project_goal: AIOS — AI-native Linux distribution. Implement the spec contracts from rev.2 (53 sub-specs CONTRACT-grade) into working Rust + Python + KDE code, following §22 MVP golden path discipline.
current_milestone: M2 — Evidence Log skeleton (S3.1 aios-evidence crate)
current_task_title: aios-evidence crate skeleton (S3.1 evidence log client)
current_step: M1 closed; queued T-007 — bootstrap aios-evidence crate with EvidenceReceipt envelope, append-only log invariant (INV-005), and the wire-format that the Capability Runtime will use to write evidence at every lifecycle edge
completed_steps_M1: T-001: aios-action crate skeleton + 8 prefix-namespaced ID types + 5-phase FSM + 4-section envelope + 11-variant error subset; 10 unit tests; cargo build/test/clippy all pass

T-002: canonical.rs (jcs_canonicalize + blake3_hash + blake3_truncated + CanonicalError); Request::request_hash; ActionEnvelope::idempotency_hash; 14 new tests (10 → 24); no new deps added; ECMA-262 number normalization deferred to T-006 with documented limitation
T-003: extended prefix-namespace registry — 5 ULID-bodied newtypes + content-addressed TransitionPlanId; IdError::InvalidHexBody added; 8 new tests (24 → 32)

T-004: lifecycle FSM behaviour layer — ConditionType closed enum (13 variants); ActionEnvelope::transition_to / add_condition / canonical_conditions_for_phase / validate_phase_conditions; TransitionError enum; 25 new tests (32 → 57)

T-005: full error taxonomy — ActionErrorCode closed enum (35 variants); ActionError struct (code/message/retryable/cause); MAX_CAUSE_CHAIN_DEPTH=8; From<IdError>/From<TransitionError>; 19 new tests (57 → 76)

T-006: serde_jcs 0.1 adopted to workspace deps (Governor approved) — canonical.rs now delegates to RFC 8785 implementation; ECMA-262 number normalization deficit from T-002 is CLOSED (verified by golden test: numerically-equal f64 values 150.0 and 1.5e2 produce identical canonical bytes + identical request_hash); CanonicalError simplified to single Projection variant; tests/ integration target with common/mod.rs shared helpers + 3 test files (golden_envelope.rs: 8 tests, lifecycle_replay.rs: 6 tests, error_paths.rs: 12 tests = 26 integration tests); 3 hand-written golden JSON fixtures under tests/fixtures/ (envelope_success_full / envelope_policy_denied / envelope_rolled_back) — each parses, validates phase ↔ conditions, and round-trips through JCS canonical bytes lossless; M1 closed at 76 unit + 26 integration = 102 tests
discovered_constraints: Rust 1.94 toolchain available
Workspace lints set: unsafe_code forbid, unwrap/expect/panic deny, clippy::pedantic warn
§21 stack lock: serde + tokio + tonic + serde + tracing pinned at workspace level
chrono added to workspace deps (needed by S0.1 §5.1 Condition.last_transition_time and Execution timestamps)
thiserror auto-source: a field named `source: String` collides with the AsDynError trait — use `detail: String` for stringified non-error contexts
Tests need `#[allow(clippy::expect_used)]` since workspace denies expect/unwrap globally — applied at test mod level

serde_json::Map defaults to BTreeMap (preserve_order feature disabled at workspace level), giving lex-sorted keys for free — but we still rebuild maps explicitly during canonicalization to defend against future workspace config drift

serde_jcs 0.1.0 (Governor-approved T-006) is the canonical Rust RFC 8785 implementation; pulls in ryu-js for ECMA-262 number normalization; ECMA-262 number normalization deficit from T-002 is now CLOSED — numerically-equal floats produce identical canonical bytes
clippy::too_long_first_doc_paragraph fires when a macro-emitted doc comment's first paragraph spans multiple sentences; split into one-sentence summary + blank doc line + body
clippy::many_single_char_names fires at 5+ single-char locals in one scope — use descriptive names
TransitionPlanId has NO random new() constructor by design — content addressing is the invariant (S15.2 §5.3 invariant 5: byte-identical replay); adding new() would silently violate this
clippy::doc_markdown flags bare PascalCase identifiers in docs — wrap policy/condition vocabulary words in backticks; even casual mentions like `serde_json` in doc comments must be backticked
clippy::panic denies panic! in non-test code; for tests, extend the test mod's #[allow(...)] to include clippy::panic when destructuring assertions need a panic-on-mismatch arm
Execution::pending() was `const fn` in T-001 but had to drop `const` in T-004 because chrono::Utc::now() is not const — initial phase_changed_at stamp must be a wall-clock now
clippy::match_same_arms fires when arms with the same body are not consolidated; when the spec's §7.3 grouping is load-bearing for readability, scope an #[allow(...)] to the single function
clippy::redundant_clone in tests requires either consuming the value (`let _ = x.into()`) or capturing what you need *before* the consuming conversion
Inside a struct impl, `Option<Box<Self>>` is preferred over `Option<Box<SelfTypeName>>` (clippy::use_self)
clippy::type_complexity fires on `[(fn(&mut T), U); N]` array types in tests — declare a local `type FnAlias = fn(&mut T);` and use that to keep the test fixture readable

Integration tests use `tests/<name>.rs` and a shared `tests/common/mod.rs` (no Cargo.toml entry needed for sub-modules under tests/); each test target compiles with `mod common;` and the workspace fully respects `cargo test --test <name>`
Integration test fixtures live under `tests/fixtures/<name>.json`; resolve via `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR").join("tests/fixtures")` rather than relying on cwd

ActionId etc are `#[serde(transparent)]` String newtypes — JSON deserialize does NOT run the ulid validator (validation only happens via explicit `parse(&str)`); fixtures may carry any opaque string in id-typed fields and still round-trip lossless
discovered_dependencies: chrono 0.4 (std + clock + serde features, default-features = false)
serde_jcs 0.1 (Governor approved T-006 — RFC 8785 canonical JSON for request_hash + idempotency_hash; pulls in ryu-js for ECMA-262 number formatting)
last_verification_result: M1 closed (T-006 final): cargo build --workspace PASS; cargo test --workspace PASS (102 tests: 76 unit + 26 integration); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings PASS; cargo fmt --check PASS
